content.problemStatement:
It is known that in regular dice, the sum of opposite faces is 777.

A regular dice is rolled and you are given the value XXX showing on the top face. Determine the value on the bottom face.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only of each test case contains a single integer XXX - the value on the top face.


Output Format
For each test case, output the value on the bottom face.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 6) – the value shown on the top face of the dice.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the value on the bottom face of the dice, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each top face X, the bottom face is 7 - X.\n3 → 4, 1 → 6, 6 → 1.

solution.approach: For a standard die the sum of opposite faces is always 7. Therefore the bottom face value is simply 7 - X. Iterate over all test cases, read X and output 7 - X.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
